description_long: This object is a roughly triangular, jagged stone with a pale beige to light brown coloration and a visibly coarse, fractured surface. The stone features sharp edges and angular facets, with one side appearing slightly darker and more weathered than the others. A short, metallic rod protrudes from one side, suggesting it may be used as a mounting or anchoring point in an urban environment. The stone's surface is uneven, with natural chips and irregularities, giving it a rugged, unpolished appearance. Its size and mass indicate it could serve as a physical barrier or obstacle, such as a decorative or functional stone in a park or along a sidewalk. The overall impression is of a natural rock that has been minimally altered, except for the addition of the metal rod.
description: A jagged, pale beige stone with sharp edges and a protruding metal rod, featuring a rough, fractured surface and natural irregularities.
description_view_0: The stone is seen from an angle highlighting its pointed top, angular faces, and the metal rod protruding from the left side.
description_view_1: This view shows the broadest, flattest face of the stone, with the metal rod not visible and the surface texture more apparent.
description_view_2: The stone is viewed edge-on, emphasizing its thin profile and the full length of the metal rod extending horizontally.
description_view_3: The back of the stone is visible, showing a darker, rougher surface and the base of the metal rod embedded in the stone.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or anchored with the metal rod into the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular, fractured pattern with natural chips and angular facets; no artificial carvings or regular veining.
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished with visible chips and some darker weathered areas, indicating moderate exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or manicured urban surfaces, but can blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements when used as a barrier or decorative feature.
